question: In the context of neural network architectures used in NLP, what is the role of the Encoder that is part of the BioBERT model?

options.A: It functions as an initial preprocessing module that prepares data for the fully connected layer in the BioBERT model.
options.B: It serves as an output layer, converting the BioBERT model's features into final predictions.
options.C: It directly interfaces with the BioBERT model to enhance the input data without any preprocessing.
options.D: It processes input data through a fully connected layer before utilizing the BioBERT model.

explanation: The Encoder follows the fully connected layer and is part of the BioBERT model, indicating its role in processing input data through this layer before utilizing the BioBERT model for further processing and feature extraction.
